English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Bernice Hopkins papers
Hopkins, Bernice.
23-3-3128
2.7 cubic feet. 2.7 cubic feet.
1925-1999. Correspondence, articles, photographs Correspondence, recipes, awards, articles, photographs, memorabilia, and other records of Bernice Hopkins as a student in Institution Management at Cornell, a dietitian, a lecturer in the Division of Nutritional Science at Cornell University and her work as a volunteer at Footnet. Collection material in English 4030574
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Staff
Date completed:
March 2004
EAD encoding:
Peter Martinez, March 2004
Date modified:
Kristen Reichenbach, January 2019
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Cornell University Class of 1932.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence, recipes, awards, articles, photographs, memorabilia, and other records of Bernice Hopkins as a student in Institution Management at Cornell, a dietitian, a lecturer in the Division of Nutritional Science at Cornell University and her work as a volunteer at Footnet.
Cite As:
Bernice Hopkins papers, #23-3-3128. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Related Materials
See also collections #47-10-3394 and #23-2-749 for additional biographical information.
Photographs. Dietitians. Cornell University -- : Faculty. Cornell University -- : Students. New York State College of Human Ecology New York State College of Home Economics New York State College of Home Economics. Department of Institution Management Foodnet (Ithaca, N.Y.) Cornell University. Class of 1932

Board Member Emeritus1989-06-09132Rotary Foundation names Bernice Hopkins a Paul Harris Fellow - certificate19901Ithaca Sunrise Rotary Club - Bernice Hopkins - Honorary Rotarian plaque1990-19911Metal box of recipes from the United States Navy2VUMC Dietetic Internship Director-gold plate1949-19602Bernice Hopkins-Retired from the New York State College of Human Ecology at Cornell University - silver plate1974-06-192Bernice Hopkins - The American Dietetic Association - glass plaque2Paul Harris Fellow medal2Envelope of various name badges and ribbons for Bernice Hopkins2Slide show-"Planning your Community Kitchen-A Cornell Slide Set"2Lace knitted by B.H.'s Grandmother1955-19592Lace knitted by B.H.'s Aunt Carrie1955-19592Pink and white flowered bag "Ditty Bag." Note by Bernice Hopkins - given me by 'Red Cross' when I went overseas in 1945. It had many things, a pocket book (to read) comb toothpaste -etc, things an overseas military person could use."1955-195922 cream colored towels. Note by Bernice Hopkins -"These towels were wedding presents to my father and mother-They were married October 10, I think, 1906." Note by B.H.1955-19592Pink lacy jacket. Note by Bernice Hopkins - "I left the Panama Canal Zone in 1945 after 11 years. The daughter of our 'clerk' in the dietary department made this jacket for me. -To this day it has never been worn. De Dier (clerk) was from Martinique-an extremely intelligent, helpful person."2Red beret witha 32 patch on it.2Wooden keepsake box with two compartments. Non-paper items have been left in the box. Folders 22-31 in Box 2 contain paper items removed from it.3Mounted portrait of Bernice Hopkins in Army UniformFolder 1Army Medical Center Junior Dietitians Course Completion Certificate1933-06-29Folder 1Bernice Hopkins' Bachelor of Science Diploma from Cornell UniversityFolder 1Helen Bull Vandervort -Human Ecology Alumni Achievement Award19964Tompkins County Trust Co. Award for Excellence199941Reunion material for the class of 1932, including the commencement program1982-199542Recognition of being a member of the American Dietetic Association for fifty years199243Biographical file - letters, articles, resume44Alumni Association constitutions and bylaws1950-198945Various memos concerning the College of Human Ecology46Division of Nutritional Sciences - memos1980-199947Passports48Correspondence concerning the History of the Department of Institution Management198549Chapters written for the History of the Department of Institution Management that were not published1991410Information gathered when writing the History of the Department of Institution Management Undated411Certificates from the College of Human Ecology Alumni Association4Photos of quilts made by the Bliss and Van Alstyne Families in the mid 1800s4Bernice Hopkins -
